summary: Developer asks why manual modifications to C# classes are still needed if all classes are auto-generated, and whether those edits will be lost on regeneration — already answered by the maintainer.

repoLinks.description: PR that introduced auto-generation of P/Invoke interop layer referenced in the issue
Manual modification commit to generated C# classes that prompted the question

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The question was about the 2019 state of the generation tooling. The maintainer answered it fully in comment #569015184, clarifying that only SkiaApi.generated.cs is auto-generated, not the wrapper classes.

analysis.summary: The reporter confused two distinct layers: SkiaApi.generated.cs (the auto-generated P/Invoke interop layer produced by PR #992) and the manually-written C# wrapper classes (e.g. SKImage.cs, SKCanvas.cs). The maintainer confirmed that only the P/Invoke declarations are generated; the higher-level C# API classes are hand-crafted for API stability and compatibility, and manual edits to them are not lost on regeneration.
analysis.rationale: Title and body clearly indicate a how/why architecture question, not a defect. The maintainer provided a complete explanation in the single comment. Issue is in 'open' state but is functionally resolved.

keySignals.text: Why do we still need to manually modify the generated C# classes like this?
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter misunderstands the layered generation: only SkiaApi.generated.cs is auto-generated; the C# wrapper classes are deliberately hand-written.

keySignals.text: So, this is partially true - the C# interop is generated, but not the classes.
keySignals.interpretation: Directly addresses and resolves the confusion: P/Invoke layer is generated, wrapper classes are not.

keySignals.text: If the C# API is 100% generated, this may be harder to preserve [compatibility].
keySignals.interpretation: Explains the intentional design decision to keep wrapper classes hand-written for ABI stability.

codeInvestigation.finding: File begins with standard using directives and type aliases; the entire file is auto-generated P/Invoke declarations. No business logic or API design — confirms it is the generated layer being discussed.
Manually-written C# wrapper class with deliberate API design, parameter validation, and helper methods. Not auto-generated — illustrates the hand-crafted layer that absorbs C++ API changes.

workarounds: The concern is unfounded: manual edits to the wrapper classes (SKImage.cs, SKCanvas.cs, etc.) are safe and will not be overwritten by the generator. Only SkiaApi.generated.cs is regenerated.

resolution.hypothesis: Reporter misunderstood the two-layer code generation architecture. The P/Invoke interop (SkiaApi.generated.cs) is generated; the C# wrapper classes are hand-written and preserve API compatibility.

proposals.title: Answer and close as not a bug
proposals.description: Summarize the maintainer's existing answer: only SkiaApi.generated.cs is auto-generated. The C# wrapper classes are intentionally hand-crafted for stability and compatibility. Manual edits to wrapper classes are safe.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Answer and close as not a bug
resolution.recommendedReason: The question is already fully answered by the maintainer. Closing avoids backlog noise.

actionability.reason: The question has been fully answered by the maintainer in December 2019. Only the P/Invoke layer is generated; C# wrapper classes are hand-crafted by design for ABI stability.

actions.description: Post clarifying answer summarizing the two-layer architecture
actions.comment:
Thanks for the question! To clarify the architecture: **only `SkiaApi.generated.cs`** (the P/Invoke interop declarations) is auto-generated by the tooling from PR #992. The higher-level C# wrapper classes (`SKImage.cs`, `SKCanvas.cs`, etc.) are deliberately hand-crafted.

This is intentional — the hand-written API layer absorbs the wild changes Google makes to the underlying Skia C++ library and maintains a stable, compatible public API for SkiaSharp consumers. Regenerating `SkiaApi.generated.cs` will **not** overwrite or lose any manual edits to the wrapper classes.

The maintainer's comment above explains the full picture: full generation of the C# API layer is a long-term goal, but requires a significantly smarter generator to preserve compatibility guarantees.
